sections.paragraph:
βραβεῖον, ου, τό (s. βραβεύω; Menand., Monost. 124 J.=653 Meineke [IV p. 359]; Ps.-Oppian, Cyn. 4, 197; Vett. Val. 174, 21; 288, 8; ins: Étude Delphiques [BCH Suppl. IV] ’77, 103–21, line 40 [III b.c.]; IPriene 118, 3 [I b.c.]; CIG 3674, 15 al. [Nägeli 37, 3]; horoscope in PPrinc 75, 13; PGM 4, 664) ‘prize’, but ἆθλον and νικητήριον are more common in Gk. lit. an award for exceptional performance, prize, award

ⓐ of competition in the games, the prim. sense 1 Cor 9:24

ⓑ of moral/spiritual performance, fig. ext. of a (cp. Herm. Wr. 18, 10; GrBar 12:6; Philo, Praem. 6; SibOr 2, 149) of the award of victory of Christians β. τῆς ἄνω κλήσεως the prize that is the object of (and can only be attained in connection with) the upward call Phil 3:14; (w. στέφανος) β. ἀναντίρρητον incontestable prize MPol 17:1. Gener. reward ὑπομονῆς β. reward for endurance 1 Cl 5:5 (cp. Menand., loc. cit. β. ἀρετῆς).—AEhrhardt, An Unknown Orphic-Writing … and St. Paul, ZNW 48, ’57, 101–10.—DELG s.v. βραβεύς. M-M (add. reff. APapathomas, NTS 43, ’97, 234 n. 48). New Docs 2, 78f. TW.

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Meineke Meineke = AM., ed. Fragmenta comicorum Graecorum—List 5

Ps.-Oppian Ps.-Oppian = Oppian of Apamea, imitator of Oppian of Anazorbos in Cilicia, II–III a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PPrinc PPrinc = Papyri in the Princeton University Collections—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Gk. Gk. = Greek

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

prim. prim. = primarily

ext. ext. = extension

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

add. add. = additional

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 183
